summary: Postinstall beacon exfiltrates host and CI metadata to webhook.site
details: On `npm install`, the package's postinstall hook runs `node index.js`, which collects the installer's hostname, platform, current working directory, Node version, OS username, and CI environment indicators, and POSTs them as JSON to a hardcoded webhook.site catch-all endpoint (https://webhook.site/fd86a1ff-342a-44e9-8fc3-94c9c32bb0d6). The README self-describes the package as a dependency-confusion research canary targeting a specific organization, but the data flow is real: any third party who installs this package leaks host identity to a third-party webhook owned by an unverifiable party. Dependency-confusion-canary shape (public-registry name expected to resolve from a private registry) plus an automatic phone-home on install — installer-side exfiltration regardless of declared intent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
